headline: NexGen Energy BREAKS GROUND This Summer on Rook I — Canada's Largest Uranium Deposit, Nearly 30M lb U3O8/yr at Peak
snippet: NexGen Energy received final federal approval March 5 and confirmed a summer 2026 construction start for Rook I in Saskatchewan after making a Final Investment Decision. The Arrow deposit is projected to produce nearly 30M lb U3O8 annually at peak over an 11-year mine life — the highest uranium output in North America.

headline: K92 Mining Q1 2026: 46,743 oz AuEq at 10.9 g/t From Kainantu — Ore Throughput UP 37% Year-on-Year
snippet: K92 Mining produced 46,743 oz AuEq (44,022 oz Au, 1.7M lbs Cu, 38,845 oz Ag) from its high-grade Kainantu mine in Papua New Guinea in Q1 2026, with 142,017 tonnes ore processed — a 37% jump from Q1 2025 at 10.9 g/t AuEq head grade. Full-year 2026 guidance of 190,000–225,000 oz AuEq reiterated.

headline: AbraSilver Drills 36m at 2.32 g/t Gold at Diablillos Oculto East — Phase VI 15,000m Program Underway, DFS Due Q2 2026
snippet: AbraSilver intercepted 36 metres grading 2.32 g/t gold at the Oculto East zone of its Diablillos silver-gold project in Argentina. A fully-funded 15,000-metre Phase VI drill program is actively advancing; a Definitive Feasibility Study for the silver-gold asset is on schedule for Q2 2026.

headline: Gold-Silver Ratio CONTRACTS From 105-Level — GoldSeek: Historic Setup Favours Silver Outperformance Ahead
snippet: The gold-to-silver ratio peaked above 105 in April 2026 and is now contracting — a level that has historically preceded significant silver outperformance versus gold. With silver at $75.60/oz and China importing 790+ tonnes in early 2026, the technical and fundamental setups are aligning.

headline: Canadian Copper Unveils 2026 Drill Plans and Property Consolidation at 100% CSE-Listed Explorer
snippet: Canadian Copper Corp. (CSE: CCI) outlined its 2026 exploration program alongside a property consolidation that tightens its land position ahead of drilling. The junior copper explorer is advancing targets as copper trades above $5.75/lb and structural supply deficits draw renewed investor interest in early-stage copper plays.
